verses.text: To the priests, Aaron's sons: be not defiled for the dead, except mother, father, son, daughter, brother, virgin sister. Make not bald, shave not the beard's corners, cut not the flesh. Holy unto God, not profane His name; they offer the bread of God — therefore holy. Guidance: Priests live closer to the holy and must mourn differently. Pagan mourners cut themselves and shaved their heads. God's priests keep their bodies whole — even grief is shaped by reverence.

Take not a profane wife, or a whore, or divorced; he is holy unto his God. Sanctify him — he offers the bread of thy God; holy to thee, for I the LORD that sanctify you am holy. Guidance: The priest's marriage is also his ministry. The home he keeps must mirror the altar he serves. Holiness reaches into the bedroom because the man who carries God's bread cannot carry shame in his house.

Take not a profane wife, or a whore, or divorced; he is holy unto his God. Sanctify him — he offers the bread of thy God; holy to thee, for I the LORD that sanctify you am holy. Guidance: The priest's marriage is also his ministry. The home he keeps must mirror the altar he serves. Holiness reaches into the bedroom because the man who carries God's bread cannot carry shame in his house.

The daughter of any priest, if she profane herself by playing the whore, profanes her father — burnt with fire. Guidance: A priest's daughter's choices echo louder than another woman's. The household carries the holiness of the altar; a grown daughter's life still belongs to a story bigger than herself. Heavy responsibility for the priestly family.

The high priest — anointed, head not uncovered, clothes not rent. Approach no dead body; not defile himself even for father or mother. Go not from the sanctuary — the anointing oil is on him. Take a virgin of his own people. Not widow, divorced, profane, harlot. Guidance: The high priest mourns no one — not even his parents — because he can never step out of his calling. Jesus carries this same intensity: a priest forever, set apart by something deeper than family ties.

The LORD: none of Aaron's seed with blemish shall offer the bread of God. Blind, lame, flat nose, broken-footed or handed, crookbackt, dwarf, eye blemish, scurvy, scabbed — not approach. He may eat the bread, most holy and holy; not enter the vail or come near altar. Guidance: Hard to read with modern eyes. The unblemished priest pictures the one Priest to come — Jesus, without spot. Blemished priests still eat the priestly bread; they are loved, fed, included. Just not at the altar.
